pattern: A single post-order recursion returns each subtree's deepest-leaf depth together with its candidate ancestor, and a node becomes the answer exactly when its two subtrees reach the same maximum depth.
hints: The answer is the deepest node from which both of the deepest leaves are still reachable, so it sits where the two deepest branches meet.
Have each call report two things: how deep its deepest leaf is, and the lowest common ancestor of its own deepest leaves.
If a node's left and right subtrees report equal depth, this node is the LCA; otherwise propagate up the deeper side's answer and depth+1.

edge_inputs.description: two deepest leaves meet at an internal node
single node is its own answer
unbalanced tree where the deeper side dominates
perfectly balanced tree returns the root
left-skewed chain returns the deepest leaf

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes and distinct values
stress.note: values distinct so the serialized answer subtree is unambiguous
